rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (just an LGTM approval), so both candidates correctly find no real bugs and both verify the rename consistently across all 9 sites, matching upstream — recall is trivially full for both. X's APPROVE verdict aligns cleanly with ground truth's 'lgtm' and its verification is well-grounded (checked upstream basic.py:23, confirmed no missed occurrences, confirmed constructor untouched). Y's single 'minor' suggestion loses precision because it recommends adding tests for create_speech/create_speech_batch/create_audio_generate error paths that the diff already shows were added/updated (hunks 7-9, which Y itself validated as 'test mock assignments updated consistently') — a self-contradictory, redundant ask. Neither has a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
